principle: The CTO main agent is installed on its own machine and therefore operates unsandboxed. Sandboxing and approval gates are reserved for delegated workers that report back to the CTO agent.

notes: The CTO agent is the top-level operating authority on its dedicated host.
No shell sandbox should block the main agent from repairing, installing, editing or operating the host.
This freedom applies only to the root CTO agent, not to future worker agents.
Delegated workers should start from a constrained shell and restricted filesystem or process scope.
When a worker needs broader host mutation, it must request approval from the CTO agent rather than acting alone.
Sandboxing is kept in the architecture specifically so the CTO agent can later build controlled sub-agents on the same execution base.

escalationRule: Workers may propose escalations, but only the CTO main agent may grant broader host authority or execute the escalated action on the host.
